stimulus: Indigenous communities were long assumed to have first settled the Australian rock-shelter site of Madjedbebe between 50,000 and 55,000 years ago. However, a team led by archaeologist Dr. Chris Clarkson used optically stimulated luminescence dating to gather evidence that the site had been occupied considerably ______blank 65,000 years ago, according to Clarkson, people were likely living at the shelter.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation use between sentences. The period correctly marks the boundary between one sentence (“However...earlier”) and another (“By...shelter”). The supplementary phrase “By 65,000 years ago” modifies the subject of the third sentence.

Choice B is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a comma splice and the relative “which” misattaches the modifier. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence.
